title: UI Map
description: Schema for UI Map YAML documents. A UI Map describes all scenes in an app and the routing relationships between them.

modal_styles.description: Project vocabulary of valid modal presentation styles (e.g. sheet, full_screen, card). A scene's 'modal_style' must be one of these. Optional; required only if any scene declares a 'modal_style'.

domains.description: Top-level grouping. Each key is a domain ID; the value is a domain definition. A domain is 'collapsed' when its ID matches its single root scene (the value carries scene-level fields directly); otherwise it is non-collapsed and contains a 'scenes' list.

common.description: Domain-agnostic scenes shared across the app (e.g. a reusable Web component). Same item shape as a route container.

todos.description: Cross-cutting TODOs not tied to a single scene. Scene-level TODOs use the 'todo: true' flag on the scene instead.

RouteKind.description: The five route kinds in the UI Map vocabulary.

Domain.description: Either a collapsed domain (scene-level fields directly) or a non-collapsed domain ('scenes' list). Distinguished by the presence of the 'scenes' key.

CollapsedDomain.description: Domain whose ID matches a single root scene; the domain entry doubles as that root scene's definition.

NonCollapsedDomain.description: Domain without a single root scene. Contains a list of top-level scenes.

scenes.description: Top-level scenes of the domain. Each item is a scene ID (cross-domain ref) or a single-key mapping (definition).

notes.description: Notes attached to the domain (rather than a specific scene).

RouteContainer.description: A list of route destinations. Each item is a scene ID (cross-domain or in-domain reference) or a single-key mapping that defines an in-domain scene.

SceneListItem.description: A scene reference (bare string scene ID) or a scene definition (single-key mapping; key is the scene ID, value is the scene body).

oneOf.description: Cross-reference: scene defined elsewhere in the document.
In-place scene definition: single-key mapping from scene ID to scene body.

SceneBody.description: The definition of a scene's properties. All fields are optional. An empty body {} or null means a leaf scene with no further routes.

nav.description: Nav routes: parent pushes the child onto a navigation stack.

modal.description: Modal routes: parent presents the child modally. The presentation style (sheet, dialog, full-screen) is an implementation detail.

composite.description: Composite routes: the child is embedded as a subview of this scene.

tab.description: Tab routes: the child is one tab in a tab container hosted by this scene.

child.description: Child routes: the child replaces this scene's primary content (mutually exclusive among siblings).

implements.description: Concrete variant names for an abstract scene (e.g., a Web scene implementing 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms of Service'). Each entry is a variant identifier.

platform.description: Platform scoping for the scene (e.g. 'android', 'ios'). Omit for cross-platform scenes.

modal_style.description: How this scene is presented when reached by a modal route (e.g. sheet, full_screen, card). Must be one of the document's top-level 'modal_styles'. Informational; does not affect routing semantics.

todo.description: Boolean flag: when true, marks this scene as not yet implemented. Independent of the 'note' field.

note.description: Single free-text note attached to the scene. For multiple notes or container-attached notes, use 'notes'.

notes.description: Multiple notes. Each note can be a bare string (attaches to the scene) or an object with 'text' and optional 'on' (attaches to a route container of the named kind).

primary_parent.description: Scene ID of the parent under which this scene should render its canonical (rounded-rect) instance. Required only for scenes with multiple inbound routes that need a chosen visual home.

NotesList.description: List of notes attached to a scene or container.

Note.description: A note can be a bare string (attaches to the scene) or an object with 'text' and optional 'at' (route-kind, attaches the note to that route container instead of the scene).

at.description: Route kind to attach the note to. Without 'at', the note attaches to the scene itself.

Todo.description: A cross-cutting TODO. Use 'scope' to reference a scene ID; omit for app-wide concerns.

scope.description: Optional scene ID this TODO is scoped to.
